root_text.sn12.36:2.1: ‘Katamaṁ jarāmaraṇaṁ, kassa ca panidaṁ jarāmaraṇan’ti iti vā, bhikkhave, yo vadeyya, ‘aññaṁ jarāmaraṇaṁ, aññassa ca panidaṁ jarāmaraṇan’ti iti vā, bhikkhave, yo vadeyya, ubhayametaṁ ekatthaṁ byañjanameva nānaṁ.
root_text.sn12.36:2.2: ‘Taṁ jīvaṁ taṁ sarīraṁ’ iti vā, bhikkhave, diṭṭhiyā sati brahmacariyavāso na hoti.
root_text.sn12.36:2.3: ‘Aññaṁ jīvaṁ aññaṁ sarīraṁ’ iti vā, bhikkhave, diṭṭhiyā sati brahmacariyavāso na hoti.
root_text.sn12.36:2.4: Ete te, bhikkhave, ubho ante anupagamma majjhena tathāgato dhammaṁ deseti:
root_text.sn12.36:3.10: katame saṅkhārā, kassa ca panime saṅkhārāti iti vā, bhikkhave, yo vadeyya, ‘aññe saṅkhārā aññassa ca panime saṅkhārā’ti iti vā, bhikkhave, yo vadeyya, ubhayametaṁ ekatthaṁ byañjanameva nānaṁ.
root_text.sn12.36:3.11: ‘Taṁ jīvaṁ taṁ sarīraṁ’ iti vā, bhikkhave, diṭṭhiyā sati brahmacariyavāso na hoti.
root_text.sn12.36:3.12: ‘Aññaṁ jīvaṁ aññaṁ sarīraṁ’ iti vā, bhikkhave, diṭṭhiyā sati brahmacariyavāso na hoti.
root_text.sn12.36:3.13: Ete te, bhikkhave, ubho ante anupagamma majjhena tathāgato dhammaṁ deseti:
root_text.sn12.36:4.1: Avijjāya tveva, bhikkhave, asesavirāganirodhā yānissa tāni visūkāyikāni visevitāni vipphanditāni kānici kānici.
root_text.sn12.36:4.2: ‘Katamaṁ jarāmaraṇaṁ, kassa ca panidaṁ jarāmaraṇaṁ’ iti vā, ‘aññaṁ jarāmaraṇaṁ, aññassa ca panidaṁ jarāmaraṇaṁ’ iti vā, ‘taṁ jīvaṁ taṁ sarīraṁ’ iti vā, ‘aññaṁ jīvaṁ, aññaṁ sarīraṁ’ iti vā.
root_text.sn12.36:4.3: Sabbānissa tāni pahīnāni bhavanti ucchinnamūlāni tālāvatthukatāni anabhāvaṅkatāni āyatiṁ anuppādadhammāni.
root_text.sn12.36:5.1: Avijjāya tveva, bhikkhave, asesavirāganirodhā yānissa tāni visūkāyikāni visevitāni vipphanditāni kānici kānici.
root_text.sn12.36:5.11: ‘katame saṅkhārā, kassa ca panime saṅkhārā’ iti vā, ‘aññe saṅkhārā, aññassa ca panime saṅkhārā’ iti vā; ‘taṁ jīvaṁ taṁ sarīraṁ’ iti vā, ‘aññaṁ jīvaṁ aññaṁ sarīraṁ’ iti vā.
root_text.sn12.36:5.12: Sabbānissa tāni pahīnāni bhavanti ucchinnamūlāni tālāvatthukatāni anabhāvaṅkatāni āyatiṁ anuppādadhammānī”ti.

translation_text.sn12.36:1.3: Choices are a requirement for consciousness. …
translation_text.sn12.36:1.4: That is how this entire mass of suffering originates.
translation_text.sn12.36:2.1: Mendicants, you might say, ‘What are old age and death, and who do they belong to?’ Or you might say, ‘Old age and death are one thing, who they belong to is another.’ But both of these mean the same thing, only the phrasing differs.
translation_text.sn12.36:2.2: If you have the view that the soul and the body are one and the same, there is no living of the spiritual life.
translation_text.sn12.36:2.3: If you have the view that the soul is one thing, the body another, there is no living of the spiritual life.
translation_text.sn12.36:2.4: Avoiding these two extremes, the Realized One teaches by the middle way:
translation_text.sn12.36:2.5: ‘Rebirth is a requirement for old age and death.’
translation_text.sn12.36:3.10: You might say, ‘What are choices, and who do they belong to?’ Or you might say, ‘Choices are one thing, who they belong to is another.’ But both of these mean the same thing, only the phrasing differs.
translation_text.sn12.36:3.11: If you have the view that the soul and the body are one and the same, there is no living of the spiritual life.
translation_text.sn12.36:3.12: If you have the view that the soul is one thing, the body another, there is no living of the spiritual life.
translation_text.sn12.36:3.13: Avoiding these two extremes, the Realized One teaches by the middle way:
translation_text.sn12.36:4.1: When ignorance fades away and ceases with nothing left over, then any twists, ducks, and dodges are given up:
translation_text.sn12.36:4.2: ‘What are old age and death, and who do they belong to?’ or ‘old age and death are one thing, who they belong to is another’, or ‘the soul and the body are one and the same’, or ‘the soul is one thing, the body another’.
translation_text.sn12.36:4.3: These are all cut off at the root, made like a palm stump, obliterated, and unable to arise in the future.
translation_text.sn12.36:5.1: When ignorance fades away and ceases with nothing left over, then any twists, ducks, and dodges are given up:
translation_text.sn12.36:5.11: ‘What are choices, and who do they belong to?’ or ‘choices are one thing, who they belong to is another’, or ‘the soul and the body are one and the same’, or ‘the soul is one thing, the body another’.
translation_text.sn12.36:5.12: These are all cut off at the root, made like a palm stump, obliterated, and unable to arise in the future.”
